search_queries_used: site:who.int ICRC Basic Emergency Care open wound laceration assessment contamination foreign body perfusion
site:dha.gov.ae wound emergency clinical guideline UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic wound closure, irrigation, debridement or foreign-body removal
automatic tetanus, antibiotic, analgesia, dressing or referral advice
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Procedure selection depends on wound-specific assessment and clinician competence.
No medicine, prophylaxis, dose, procedure or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with open wounds or lacerations; bites, burns, penetrating injuries, major trauma, surgical wounds and high-risk hosts require distinct evidence.
UAE_applicability: WHO documentation domains transfer, but UAE infection-control, tetanus, rabies, antimicrobial, consent, procedure, occupational and referral policy governs.
recency_verification: WHO/ICRC Basic Emergency Care 2018 exact wound and trauma sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: No newer WHO/ICRC BEC edition was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact WHO/ICRC open-wound section supports onset and mechanism context, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, clinician risk context, focused wound and distal-perfusion examination, appearance and existing procedure status only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports laceration or abrasion, location, size and depth, bleeding, contamination, foreign-body concern, underlying structures, distal perfusion, dressing, splinting, tetanus, and return or disposition documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact SAMPLE history and disposition material supports patient concerns, measured vital signs, reviewed tests, clinician plan, discussion and arranged handover or follow-up without selecting wound closure, tetanus, antibiotics or disposition.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The trauma secondary survey was reviewed for injury context; only the generic manual focused-assessment fields were mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.

unresolved_source_gaps: Wound site, size, depth, contamination, foreign body, neurovascular and tendon status, bite or exposure, tetanus status, imaging, closure, procedure, medicine, dressing and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
